resolved_defects.evidence: Windows PowerShell 5.1 real-world regression stopped during the temporary-clone apply because the accumulated-lessons governance Markdown file was absent from the canonical Master Asset Register.
resolved_defects.root_cause: RC1 synthetic tests invented Master Asset Register rows for three predecessor governance control files and therefore did not reproduce the actual Build 0052 register topology.
resolved_defects.impact: RC1 could not pass the mandatory Windows PowerShell 5.1 regression and is prohibited from import. The canonical repository was not modified because transactional rollback returned ROLLBACK_STATE_EXACT.
resolved_defects.corrective_action: Classify the three generated governance control updates as NON_ASSET_ADMIN with Master Asset Register action NO_CHANGE; preserve transactional byte updates, predecessor overlap controls and rollback ownership.
resolved_defects.preventive_control: Synthetic predecessor fixtures must reproduce canonical formal-asset registration topology and must not invent register rows for predecessor paths.
resolved_defects.executable_regression: test_build_0053_transactional_import.py now runs with the governance files present in Git but absent from the Master Asset Register and requires forced rollback plus clean reapply success.

resolved_defects.evidence: Windows PowerShell 5.1 temporary-clone regression reported raw working-tree/index byte mismatches for ASSET_REGISTER_CHANGE_REPORT.json, CANONICAL_IMPORT_REPORT.json, POST_IMPORT_REPOSITORY_VALIDATION.json and PREDECESSOR_CANONICAL_EVIDENCE.json.
resolved_defects.root_cause: Runtime Python Path.write_text calls omitted an explicit newline policy. On Windows, newline=None translated LF to CRLF in the working tree, while Git normalised staged text to LF.
resolved_defects.impact: RC2 could not pass the mandatory raw staged-byte equality gate and is prohibited from canonical import. Failure occurred in the temporary clone before canonical apply.
resolved_defects.corrective_action: Force newline="\n" on every production Python write_text call, including importer reports, repository validation reports, predecessor evidence, preflight outputs, byte reports and dashboard output.
resolved_defects.preventive_control: AST regression rejects any production Python write_text call without an explicit LF newline argument; Windows PowerShell 5.1 regression retains exact staged-byte equality after runtime report generation.
resolved_defects.executable_regression: test_build_0053_repository.py audits all production Python write_text calls; temporary-clone raw staged-byte equality remains mandatory.

resolved_defects.evidence: RC3 passed the temporary-clone Windows PowerShell 5.1 regression, then four post-import tests failed in the canonical repository because they scanned historical repository content rather than exact Build 0053 ownership. The canonical transaction was restored manually to Build 0052 with BUILD_0053_RC3_ROLLBACK_STATE_EXACT.
resolved_defects.root_cause: The regression did not execute the complete Build 0053 test suite before canonical apply. Four tests used repository-wide globbing or recursive scans instead of the Asset Intent Manifest and package inventory.
resolved_defects.impact: RC3 applied 91 uncommitted paths before post-import validation failed. No commit or push occurred, and exact transactional rollback restored the clean Build 0052 baseline.
resolved_defects.corrective_action: Scope all Build 0053 tests to manifest-owned package paths and examples; execute repository validation and the full Build 0053 test suite in the temporary clone before canonical apply.
resolved_defects.preventive_control: The operator runner automatically restores the canonical transaction backup if any post-import validation, test, staging, Git hygiene or staged-byte gate fails after apply.
resolved_defects.executable_regression: test_post_import_failure_rollback_restores_staged_repository proves exact restoration after a successful apply and staging; the Windows PowerShell 5.1 regression requires BUILD_0053_PRE_CANONICAL_FULL_SUITE_VALIDATED before commit/push simulation.

constraints: No private repository byte access in production environment; predecessor bytes are therefore derived and verified at operator runtime from the canonical Git commit.
